connections.title: Agnus or agnum?
connections.teaser: See how Lesson I appears in the Mass.
connections.latin: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi…
connections.english: Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world…
explanation: In Father Most’s lesson, agnum means ‘lamb’ when the lamb is receiving the action.
In Agnus Dei, the form is agnus because the Lamb of God is the one performing the action: taking away the sins of the world.
The change is small, but it shows why Latin endings matter. They help tell us what job a word is doing in the sentence.

forms.meaning: something is being done to the lamb

connections.exercise: Can you now see why the prayer begins with agnus rather than agnum?
connections.sourceLabel: See the Agnus Dei in an official Vatican liturgical resource
